evals.prompt: Create a ragbits RAG application called policy-qa over HR policy documents. CLI only — no web UI.
evals.expected_output: A policy-qa/ directory with pyproject.toml (ragbits-core + ragbits-document-search), README.md, documents/sample.md, policy_qa/__init__.py, policy_qa/ingest.py (LiteLLMEmbedder + InMemoryVectorStore + DocumentSearch + cached factory with auto-ingest), and policy_qa/search.py (CLI loop that calls get_document_search(), retrieves context, and asks LiteLLM for an answer). Domain-specific system prompt. Dependencies installed.

assertions.name: project_directory_created
assertions.description: policy-qa/ directory exists

assertions.name: ingest_file_exists
assertions.description: policy_qa/ingest.py exists and imports LiteLLMEmbedder, DocumentSearch, DocumentMeta, and InMemoryVectorStore

assertions.name: search_file_exists
assertions.description: policy_qa/search.py exists, imports get_document_search from policy_qa.ingest, and calls LiteLLM.generate with a grounded system prompt

assertions.name: no_app_file
assertions.description: policy_qa/app.py does NOT exist (chat-ui was not requested)

assertions.name: init_file_exists
assertions.description: policy_qa/__init__.py exists so the directory is a valid package

assertions.name: sample_document_present
assertions.description: documents/sample.md exists so the app can ingest something on first run

assertions.name: pyproject_has_core_and_doc_search
assertions.description: pyproject.toml lists ragbits-core AND ragbits-document-search as dependencies

assertions.name: in_memory_factory_caches_and_autoingests
assertions.description: ingest.py has a module-level _document_search cache and auto-ingests from DOCUMENTS_DIR on first call (the in-memory ephemerality fix)

assertions.name: system_prompt_is_domain_specific
assertions.description: The SYSTEM_PROMPT references HR, policy, or an equivalent domain — not a generic placeholder

assertions.name: readme_exists
assertions.description: README.md exists with install, ingest, and run instructions

evals.prompt: Build a ragbits knowledge base called docs-bot with a web chat UI, persistent Chroma vector store, and an LLM reranker to improve retrieval quality.
evals.expected_output: A docs-bot/ directory. pyproject.toml depends on ragbits-core[chroma], ragbits-document-search, and ragbits-chat. ingest.py uses ChromaVectorStore (no in-memory cache, no auto-ingest) and wires a LiteLLMReranker into DocumentSearch. app.py subclasses ChatInterface, implements chat() that awaits get_document_search(), yields references, and streams the LLM response. Main block launches RagbitsAPI(RAGChat).run().

assertions.name: project_directory_created
assertions.description: docs-bot/ directory exists

assertions.name: chroma_extra_declared
assertions.description: pyproject.toml depends on ragbits-core[chroma] (with the chroma extra), not plain ragbits-core

assertions.name: chat_package_declared
assertions.description: pyproject.toml lists ragbits-chat

assertions.name: app_file_exists
assertions.description: docs_bot/app.py exists, subclasses ChatInterface, and defines an async chat() method

assertions.name: no_search_file
assertions.description: docs_bot/search.py does NOT exist (chat-ui supersedes the CLI loop)

assertions.name: reranker_wired_in
assertions.description: ingest.py imports LiteLLMReranker from ragbits.document_search.retrieval.rerankers.litellm and passes reranker=... to DocumentSearch

assertions.name: chroma_vector_store_used
assertions.description: ingest.py imports ChromaVectorStore and constructs it with chromadb.PersistentClient (no in-memory fallback)

assertions.name: chat_yields_references
assertions.description: chat() calls self.create_reference(...) for retrieved elements before streaming the answer

assertions.name: chat_streams_response
assertions.description: chat() uses llm.generate_streaming and yields self.create_text_response(chunk) in a loop

assertions.name: main_block_launches_ragbitsapi
assertions.description: The __main__ block imports RagbitsAPI and calls RagbitsAPI(RAGChat).run()

evals.prompt: Create a ragbits RAG app called support-kb with a chat UI, login required, like/dislike feedback forms, branded header with a domain-appropriate logo, and a Qdrant vector store.
evals.expected_output: A support-kb/ directory. pyproject.toml uses ragbits-core[qdrant] + ragbits-document-search + ragbits-chat. ingest.py uses QdrantVectorStore with AsyncQdrantClient. app.py defines LikeForm/DislikeForm Pydantic models, a get_auth_backend() factory with ListAuthenticationBackend + InMemorySessionStore, RAGChat with feedback_config, ui_customization (title, subtitle, welcome_message, starter_questions), and a __main__ block that calls RagbitsAPI(RAGChat, auth_backend=get_auth_backend()).run(). README documents the seed credentials.

assertions.name: project_directory_created
assertions.description: support-kb/ directory exists

assertions.name: qdrant_extra_declared
assertions.description: pyproject.toml depends on ragbits-core[qdrant]

assertions.name: qdrant_vector_store_used
assertions.description: ingest.py imports QdrantVectorStore and AsyncQdrantClient and uses them in get_document_search()

assertions.name: auth_backend_factory_present
assertions.description: app.py defines get_auth_backend() that returns a ListAuthenticationBackend with an InMemorySessionStore and at least one seed user

assertions.name: feedback_forms_defined
assertions.description: app.py defines LikeForm and DislikeForm Pydantic models with json_schema_serialization_defaults_required=True

assertions.name: feedback_config_attached
assertions.description: RAGChat has a feedback_config class attribute referencing LikeForm and DislikeForm

assertions.name: ui_customization_domain_specific
assertions.description: ui_customization has non-placeholder title, subtitle, welcome_message, and 2+ starter_questions tied to the support-knowledge-base domain

assertions.name: main_block_passes_auth_backend
assertions.description: __main__ calls RagbitsAPI(RAGChat, auth_backend=get_auth_backend()).run() — not the default single-arg form

assertions.name: readme_documents_credentials
assertions.description: README lists the seed credentials and flags them as demo-only

assertions.name: chat_yields_references
assertions.description: chat() yields self.create_reference(...) for retrieved elements and streams the LLM answer
